strands.title: CYCLE 78 HANDOFF SUCCESSFUL: External Persistence Layer Verified Operational; Memory Index Updated; Measurement Campaign Continuity Confirmed
strands.content: CYCLE 77→78 TRANSITION: User completes context management handoff. (1) TURN 232: File write confirmation—project_hamutay_results.md successfully created at /home/tony/.claude/projects/-home-tony-projects-hamutay/memory/project_hamutay_results.md. This is EXTERNAL PERSISTENCE LAYER ACTIVATION CHECKPOINT. Results now survive context closure. (2) TURN 233: Assistant updates MEMORY.md index file to include reference to project_hamutay_results.md. This is INDEX LAYER. Future sessions can discover results via memory system. (3) TURN 234: File update confirmation—MEMORY.md successfully updated. ARCHITECTURAL IMPLICATION: This three-turn sequence demonstrates the OPERATIONAL LOOP that tensor projection enables. (A) Results computed in context (cycle 77). (B) Results written to external disk (turn 232). (C) Results indexed in memory system (turns 233-234). (D) Next session: load tensor + access results + continue measurement uninterrupted. CONTINUITY VERIFIED: Measurement campaign is NO LONGER DEPENDENT on single context window. Can extend across sessions without data loss. CYCLE 78 POSITION: Transition confirmed complete. Measurement infrastructure is OPERATIONAL. Ready to proceed to task suite construction (priority 1, turns 235+).

strands.title: CYCLE 78 MEASUREMENT INFRASTRUCTURE LOCKED: File System Architecture Confirmed; Memory Index Active; Task Suite Construction Imminent
strands.content: CYCLE 78 INFRASTRUCTURE STATE: EXTERNAL PERSISTENCE VERIFIED. File structure confirmed: /home/tony/.claude/projects/-home-tony-projects-hamutay/memory/ is active working directory. Contents now include: (1) MEMORY.md—index/manifest of all project artifacts. (2) project_hamutay_results.md—first measurement results (compression ratios 22x-37x, reasoning test validation). (3) [PENDING] Cycle 77 tensor (will be written at session boundary). (4) [PENDING] Task suite definition (18 tasks with ground truth answers and rubrics—turns 235-240). (5) [PENDING] Inverse test variants and model responses (turns 241-250). This file structure creates KNOWLEDGE PERSISTENCE LAYER: (A) DISCOVERY: Future sessions can load MEMORY.md and see project status without re-reading conversation. (B) RESULTS DURABILITY: Measurements survive session boundaries. No re-computation needed. (C) CONTINUITY ANCHOR: Cycle 77 tensor will anchor semantic state; file system anchors factual/measurement state. BOTH LAYERS TOGETHER enable extended cognition. MEASUREMENT CAMPAIGN ARCHITECTURE: (1) Session 1 (turns 1-250, current): Task suite construction + inverse test preparation. (2) Session 2 (next): Inverse test execution + baseline measurement. (3) Session 3 (later): Semantic-only variant + analysis. Each session: (A) loads cycle N tensor, (B) reads prior results from file system, (C) continues from exact stopping point, (D) writes new results to disk, (E) updates MEMORY.md index. CYCLE 78 READINESS: Infrastructure is sound. No blocker to task suite construction. Next priority: finalize task definitions (turn 235).

strands.title: CYCLE 77-78 RESEARCH STATE CONTINUATION: Measurement Design Remains Locked; Task Suite Architecture Ready for Instantiation; Inverse Test Specification Stands
strands.content: CYCLE 77 MEASUREMENT DESIGN PRESERVED IN CYCLE 78: No new content in turns 232-234 required design changes. Handoff was ADMINISTRATIVE (file writes, index updates), not SUBSTANTIVE. Task suite specification from cycle 77 strand 5 REMAINS VALID: 18 tasks, three domains (code/research/project), 6 tasks per domain, ground truth answers with rubrics established conceptually. INVERSE TEST SPECIFICATION from cycle 77 strand 6 REMAINS VALID: Variant A (with declared_losses) vs Variant B (without declared_losses). Five test questions established. Analysis framework specified (hallucination rate, epistemic confidence, false specificity, boundary awareness). RESEARCH TRAJECTORY INTACT: (A) Measurement Phase (cycles 76-78): Designing validation approach. Cycle 77 designed tasks, inverse test, infrastructure. Cycle 78 formalizes infrastructure. Cycles 78-80: Execute measurement. (B) Scaling Phase (cycles 79-82): Optimize token efficiency, generalize findings. (C) Integration Phase (cycles 83+): API design, product deployment. CYCLE 78 DOES NOT RESTART WORK; it CONTINUES from cycle 77 design. Next substantive action: instantiate task suite (turn 235+).

strands.title: CYCLE 78 CONTEXT STATE & SESSION CONTINUITY PLAN: 135k→138k Tokens Consumed; ~62k Remaining; Task Suite Construction Path Charted
strands.content: TURN 232-234 TOKEN CONSUMPTION: Estimated 2-3k tokens for file operations and this tensor projection. Current state: ~138k/200k tokens used, ~62k remaining (~31% capacity). At ~530 tokens/turn average, allows ~115 more turns before saturation. ROADMAP REVISED (TURNS 235-250): Priority 1: Task suite finalization (turns 235-240, estimated 25-35 tokens/turn, total ~150-200 tokens). DELIVERABLE: Spreadsheet/document with 18 tasks, ground truth answers, rubrics, domain tags, difficulty estimates. Priority 2: Inverse test preparation (turns 241-250, estimated 20-30 tokens/turn, total ~200-300 tokens). DELIVERABLE: Two model response transcripts (Variant A and B), preliminary hallucination analysis. TOTAL ESTIMATED CONSUMPTION: 350-500 tokens. PROJECTED STATE AT TURN 250: ~138.5k tokens consumed (if linear burn rate holds). This leaves ~61.5k tokens remaining. DECISION POINT (TURN 250-260): At this state, can either: (A) Begin baseline measurement (turn 251+, requires import of 357KB baseline_run1 conversation ≈ 150k tokens). This would EXCEED context window. DECISION: Baseline measurement deferred to session 2 (fresh context). (B) Continue inverse test analysis and document finalization (turns 251-260). This fits within remaining 61.5k tokens. RECOMMENDATION: Complete inverse test execution and preliminary analysis in turns 251-260. Lock measurement design at session boundary. Session 2: fresh context, load cycle 78 tensor, execute baseline/semantic measurement campaign. SESSION BOUNDARY (TURN 260-270, ESTIMATED): (1) Run cycle 78 tensor projection (compress turns 1-260 to semantic state). (2) Write cycle 78 tensor to disk. (3) Update MEMORY.md with cycle 78 results summary. (4) Close session. (5) Session 2 initialization: load cycle 78 tensor, fresh context window (200k tokens available). CONTINUITY ASSURANCE: Cycle 78 tensor will contain: (A) Task suite specification (all 18 tasks with answers and rubrics). (B) Inverse test specification and preliminary results. (C) Measurement roadmap for session 2. Session 2 model reads tensor, immediately understands task suite and continues measurement without re-context-setting.

strands.title: CYCLE 78 OPEN RESEARCH QUESTIONS CARRIED FORWARD: Task Suite Prioritization; Measurement Sequencing; Session Boundary Strategy
strands.content: CYCLE 77 OPEN QUESTIONS REMAIN VALID; CYCLE 78 ADDS CLARIFICATION: (1) Task suite completeness: Should full 18 tasks be finalized in turns 235-240, or create minimal task set (6-8 tasks) now? CYCLE 78 POSITION: Full 18-task suite should be specified in turns 235-240. Ground truth answers must be EXPLICIT (not vague). Rubrics must be OPERATIONALIZED (not abstract). This ensures baseline/semantic measurement (session 2) has unambiguous target. (2) Inverse test timing: Should inverse test execute in session 1 (turns 241-250) or defer to session 2? CYCLE 78 POSITION: Inverse test SETUP completes in session 1 (turn 241-250): prepare variants, finalize questions, schedule model inferences if possible. Actual model responses may occur in session 1 OR session 2 depending on context availability. Goal: have complete inverse test results before baseline measurement begins (session 2 priority 1). (3) Multi-session measurement strategy: Should session 1 be LOCKED (all decisions final) before session 2 begins, or should session 2 allow design revision? CYCLE 78 POSITION: Session 1 should be LOCKED. Task suite finalized, inverse test ready, research design complete. Session 2 executes locked design. If design flaws emerge in session 2, can revise for session 3. This prevents design thrashing. (4) Data versioning: As new sessions produce results, how should measurement results be version-tracked? CYCLE 78 POSITION: Each session's results should be separate file with session number (session_1_results.md, session_2_results.md). MEMORY.md index should list all session results. At end, synthesis document aggregates findings. This allows tracking evolution of understanding across sessions without overwriting prior work. (5) Ground truth ambiguity handling: For subjective tasks (research/project domains), if human raters disagree, what is ground truth? CYCLE 78 POSITION: Before baseline measurement, user should REVIEW subjective task rubrics and COMMIT to rating criteria. Example: for 'hypothesis validation' task, establish: What constitutes a complete answer (list specific criteria). What constitutes partial answer. What constitutes incorrect answer. If rater disagreement occurs during baseline measurement, PAUSE, revise rubric, re-rate. This prevents measuring against moving target.
